text:
A successful appeal following a tumultuous heat of the 5000-metres has given Stewart McSweyn a second chance at Olympic glory.

Loginor signup to continue reading

The 29-year-old King Islander will run in the 5000-metre final despite finishing three seconds outside the top eight at Stade de France.

Britain's George Mills caused three runners to fall in the final straight, while forcing McSweyn onto the infield, but he kept his feet and finished the race in 12thwith a time of 14:12.31.

"I'm happy I stayed up. A lot of guys hit the ground, so we'll see what happens," McSweyn said in the aftermath.

"I wasn't able to finish the race how I hoped, but I guess we've got to wait and see what the officials make of it."

McSweyn later appealed the result - and the jury agreed - advancing him to the next round several hours later.

Four other runners that were also caught up in the chaos have also been advanced, meaning 22 athletes will contest the final on Sunday, August 11 at 3.50am.

It will be McSweyn's second appearance in an Olympic final after finishing seventh in the 1500 meters at the Tokyo Olympics in 2021.

He is the only Australian to qualify for the 5000-metre final after Morgan McDonald contested the second heat and agonisingly missed out in ninth spot by 0.05 seconds.

It was part of a somewhat farcical day, which included a cameraman wandering onto the track while racing took place.

title: Stewart McSweyn reaches 5000m Olympic final after appeal | The Advocate | Burnie, TAS
description: Stewart McSweyn advanced to 5000m Paris Olympic final after a successful appeal following chaotic heat at Stade de France.
